steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. An expression in the form ${x}^{\frac{1}{k}}$, where $x>0$ and $k>0$, is equivalent to $\sqrt[k]{x}$. It follows that ${x}^{\frac{1}{14}}$, where $x>0$, is equivalent to $\sqrt[14]{x}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
